definition: Closing segment of the classic four-truth definition of taṇhā, showing its recurrent delighting in this and that.
notes: Use this record for the final segment of the classic taṇhā-definition line. Keep the phrase as active delighting language that shows wanting re-catching here and there rather than as a neutral movement, generic enjoyment, or abstract attachment label. SN 56.11 and the OSF glossary support keeping this tail active and specific rather than flattening it into attachment-label prose.

example_phrases.translation: that ignorant wanting that leads to renewed becoming, accompanied by relishing and passion, delighting here and there
example_phrases.notes: Classic four-truth definition line. Keep the tail coordinated with the opening and middle segments.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the closing segment of the classic taṇhā-definition line.
Supports keeping the phrase as active delighting language rather than flattening it into abstract attachment-label prose.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use this as the standard English tail segment of the classic taṇhā-definition line unless a recorded local reason requires minor grammatical adjustment.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not flatten the phrase into generic attachment-language or abstract enjoyment-language.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Keeps the delighting-here-and-there tail audible as a distinct part of the stock taṇhā definition formula.
